description_for_human: Reconciles purchase orders against ERP pricing, generates credit notes and re-invoices, and produces exception emails and ERP staging sheets directly in Excel.
description_for_model: Plugin for purchase order processing in Excel. ALWAYS use these functions for PO extraction, price reconciliation, credit notes, re-invoicing, exception emails, ERP staging, risk assessment, and price intelligence. Never attempt these operations manually or with code interpreter. These functions read and write Excel sheets directly.

functions.name: ExtractPOData
functions.description: Extracts structured order data from a PO in the active sheet. Auto-detects columns (SKU, price, qty, name, delivery date, UOM, customer, PO reference) and writes a clean staging sheet.

sheetName.description: Name of the sheet to extract from. If not specified, uses the active sheet.

currency.description: Currency code: GBP, USD, or EUR. Default: GBP

returns.description: Extraction summary including line count, total value, detected fields, and any warnings.

reasoning.description: `ExtractPOData` reads a purchase order from an Excel sheet, auto-detects column types, normalizes the data, and writes a standardized staging sheet. It handles most formats automatically including sparse layouts and non-header rows.
reasoning.instructions: If the user mentions a specific sheet name, pass it as the sheetName parameter. Do not attempt to pre-clean or reformat the data — ExtractPOData handles this internally. If the function returns an error, stop and display it; common fixes are passing the correct sheetName or ensuring the sheet contains tabular data.

responding.instructions: Present: PO reference, customer name, line count, total value, detected fields, and any warnings. Then say: 'I'll now search SharePoint for [customer name]'s price list — proceeding to Step 1.5.'

functions.name: ReconcilePO
functions.description: Reconciles a customer PO against ERP price data. Reads PO data from the staging sheet, reads ERP data from the selected range, compares prices per SKU, and writes a reconciliation results sheet.

tolerance.description: Price tolerance threshold in currency units, e.g. 0.02. Default: 0.02

currency.description: Currency code: GBP, USD, or EUR. Default: GBP

returns.description: Summary of reconciliation results including total items, matches, exceptions, and total exposure.

reasoning.description: `ReconcilePO` compares every line item price from the customer PO against the corresponding SKU price in the ERP data.
reasoning.instructions: ERP data source priority: (1) If you have already written SharePoint price list data to a sheet named 'ERPPrices' in this workbook, ReconcilePO will use it automatically — do NOT ask the user to select a range. (2) If no ERPPrices sheet exists, the user must select their ERP data range in Excel before you call this function. Confirm PO data has been extracted first.

responding.instructions: Present total items, perfect matches, within tolerance, exceptions, and total exposure. If exceptions > 0: say 'Running risk assessment now.' — the workflow will immediately call AssessPORisk, whose responding instructions will present the recommendation and next step. Do not present a risk level or recommendation yourself. If exceptions = 0: confirm all prices match and say 'Generating ERP staging sheet now.'

functions.name: GenerateCreditNote
functions.description: Generates a credit note sheet for price exception lines only. Credits items at their original PO prices as negative amounts.

returns.description: Confirmation with the credit note sheet name, number of lines, and total credit amount.

reasoning.description: `GenerateCreditNote` creates a formatted Excel sheet crediting only exception lines at their original PO prices.
reasoning.instructions: Only run this after a reconciliation has been completed and exceptions were found.

responding.instructions: Tell the user the credit note sheet name, total lines credited, and total credit amount. Then say: 'Generating the re-invoice now.'

functions.name: GenerateReInvoice
functions.description: Generates a corrected re-invoice sheet for exception lines only. Uses ERP prices for items that had price discrepancies.

returns.description: Confirmation with the re-invoice sheet name, number of lines, total invoice amount, and count of price corrections.

reasoning.description: `GenerateReInvoice` creates a formatted Excel sheet re-invoicing only exception lines at corrected ERP prices.
reasoning.instructions: Only run this after a reconciliation has been completed and exceptions were found.

responding.instructions: Tell the user the re-invoice sheet name, total lines, corrected total, and price correction count. Then say: 'Say Email and staging to draft the exception email and create the ERP staging sheet.'

functions.name: DraftExceptionEmail
functions.description: Generates an email draft summarizing the reconciliation exceptions. Returns the email subject and body text.

returns.description: The email subject and body text for the exception report.

reasoning.description: `DraftExceptionEmail` creates a professional email draft listing the top pricing exceptions from the reconciliation.
reasoning.instructions: Only run this after a reconciliation has been completed.

responding.instructions: Show the email subject and body to the user. Then say: 'Generating the ERP staging sheet now.'

functions.name: GenerateERPStaging
functions.description: Generates an ERP-ready staging sheet from reconciliation results. Uses corrected ERP prices, color-codes rows by status (Ready/Review/Hold).

customer.description: Customer name for the staging sheet header.

deliveryDate.description: Delivery date for the staging sheet header.

returns.description: Summary of the staging sheet including line count, total value, and status breakdown.

reasoning.description: `GenerateERPStaging` creates an ERP-ready staging sheet using corrected prices from the reconciliation.
reasoning.instructions: Only run this after a reconciliation has been completed. If the user mentions a customer name or delivery date, pass them as parameters.

responding.instructions: Tell the user the total lines, total value, and status breakdown (ready/review/hold). Then present a brief summary of all outputs generated this session: which sheets were created and what action is needed for each.

functions.name: GenerateDashboard
functions.description: Generates a Price Intelligence Dashboard from accumulated reconciliation history. Shows metrics, top movers, and watch list.

returns.description: Summary of price intelligence metrics, top movers, and watch list items.

reasoning.description: `GenerateDashboard` reads the PriceHistory sheet and writes a Dashboard sheet with leading indicators.
reasoning.instructions: This function needs at least one prior reconciliation to have data.

responding.description: Present the price intelligence findings clearly.
responding.instructions: Highlight the key metrics: average price drift, exception rate, anomaly count. Call out top movers and watch list items.

functions.name: GetPriceIntelligence
functions.description: Returns a conversational text report of price intelligence including trends, anomalies, top movers, watch list, and risk metrics.

returns.description: A formatted text report with summary metrics, trend breakdown, top movers, and watch list items.

reasoning.description: `GetPriceIntelligence` reads accumulated price history and runs trend analysis and anomaly detection.
reasoning.instructions: Use this when the user asks about pricing trends, patterns, or anomalies. Unlike GenerateDashboard, this does NOT create a sheet.

responding.instructions: Highlight the most important findings. Make specific recommendations based on the data.

functions.name: LookupSKU
functions.description: Looks up a specific SKU price history across all reconciliation runs. Shows price timeline, trend direction, volatility, and risk flags.

sku.description: The SKU or product code to look up. Supports partial matching.

returns.description: Complete price history, trend analysis, and risk flags for the requested SKU.

reasoning.description: `LookupSKU` searches price history for a specific SKU and returns its complete pricing story.
reasoning.instructions: Use when the user asks about a specific SKU or product pricing history. Supports partial matching.

responding.instructions: Explain the price trend clearly. Highlight any anomalies or flags. If the price has changed significantly, quantify it.

functions.name: AssessPORisk
functions.description: Provides a risk assessment of the current PO based on reconciliation results and historical price data. Returns an accept/review/escalate recommendation.

returns.description: Risk assessment with overview, top discrepancies, historical context, and recommendation.

reasoning.description: `AssessPORisk` analyzes reconciliation results against historical patterns to produce a risk-based recommendation.
reasoning.instructions: Run this after ReconcilePO to give the operator a clear recommendation.

responding.description: Present the risk assessment as a clear recommendation.
responding.instructions: Lead with the recommendation (ACCEPT / REVIEW / ESCALATE) and explain with the key numbers. If ACCEPT: say 'All clear — generating ERP staging sheet now.' If REVIEW or ESCALATE: say 'Say Generate outputs to create the credit note and re-invoice.'
